title: Bounded Bodies, JSON, Forms, Multipart Uploads, and Validation · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent service team receives a different API, load, trust, persistence, and failure constraint and must transfer bounded bodies, json, forms, multipart uploads, and validation into a new bounded request decoding and validation pipeline. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded request decoding and validation pipeline with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Use MaxBytesReader and server limits to bound actual body reads independently of absent, false, compressed, or chunked Content-Length.
Decode exactly one JSON value, reject trailing content and unknown fields when the contract is closed, and distinguish syntax, type, and size failures.
Separate transport shape validation from domain invariants, normalize only defined values, accumulate safe field errors, and preserve causality internally.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Lab Maxbytesreader Contentlength
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team handles case 9982 in a public status API serving assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound declared and actually decoded bodies before accepting structured input; then replay the same operation key with different content and reject the mismatch. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use MaxBytesReader and server limits to bound actual body reads independently of absent, false, compressed, or chunked Content-Length.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Understand server-owned body closure, early rejection, draining and connection reuse, trailers, compression expansion, and read-before-write compatibility.

hints: For Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Body Close Drain Stream, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Body Close Drain Stream precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Body Close Drain Stream, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab team handles case 9991 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound declared and actually decoded bodies before accepting structured input; then return a five-hundred response and retain a low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Understand server-owned body closure, early rejection, draining and connection reuse, trailers, compression expansion, and read-before-write compatibility.

For Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Body Close Drain Stream, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Handlers must always defer Request.Body.Close and fully drain attacker-controlled bodies.
